steps.title: Step 1
steps.content: Identify the three payment components: a one-time down payment of \$37, $p$ monthly payments of \$16 each (total $16p$), and a grand total of \$165.

steps.title: Step 2
steps.content: Form the equation: down payment + monthly payments = total. Substituting: $37 + 16p = 165$, which is the same as $16p + 37 = 165$.

steps.title: Step 3
steps.content: Verify by solving: $16p = 128$, so $p = 8$ monthly payments, and $8 \times 16 + 37 = 128 + 37 = 165$. Correct.

steps.title: Step 4
steps.content:
Evaluate every choice:
  • A: $16p - 37 = 165$ — subtracts the down payment instead of adding it; it would imply $p = 12.625$, not the intended payment setup. Eliminated.
  • B: $37p - 16 = 165$ — swaps the roles of 37 and 16; it would make $p \approx 4.9$. Eliminated.
  • C: $16p + 37 = 165$ — matches the derived equation exactly. That confirms C is correct.
  • D: $37p + 16 = 165$ — treats 37 as the per-payment amount and 16 as the down payment, reversing both values. Eliminated.
